objections.claim: The proposal's "no downstream dependencies" / "local blast radius" claim is false for the dashboard deep-reflect path. `dashboard-api/worker.js:289-297` loads every `experience:*` key into `periodExperiences`, and `dashboard-api/worker.js:330` reports `accumulation.experiences_total = periodExperiences.length` with no schema filter. `site/patron/src/components/ReflectionsTab.jsx:269-272` renders that value as "Experiences recorded". Deleting `experience:session_4_waiting_posture` will change historical dashboard metrics for any DR accumulation window that includes it, even though core experience selection already skips the malformed record.
objections.falsifiable_test: Call `/deep-reflect/<sessionId>` for a DR session whose accumulation period includes `experience:session_4_waiting_posture`, record `accumulation.experiences_total`, delete the key, and call the endpoint again. If the count does not decrease by 1, this objection is false.

objections.claim: Deletion hides an active schema-health signal without fixing the write surface that can recreate the problem. `scripts/analyze-sessions.mjs:109-118,166-169` exports every `experience:*` record verbatim, and `scripts/dev-loop/classify.mjs:156-168,252-264` audits those exported experiences and emits issues such as vague/malformed observations. But malformed `experience:*` writes are still possible through deep-reflect and manual tooling: `reflect.js:472-481` applies arbitrary `kv_operations`, `kernel.js:576-620,783-787` allows new unprotected agent-key writes with no experience-schema validation, and `scripts/write-kv.mjs:51-55` writes arbitrary JSON directly to KV. Deleting this one key suppresses the current warning but does not close the path that produced it.
objections.falsifiable_test: Delete `experience:session_4_waiting_posture`, then write a new malformed key such as `experience:test_bad` via deep-reflect `kv_operations` or `node scripts/write-kv.mjs experience:test_bad '{"observed":"x"}'`, rerun the analyze/classify pipeline, and check whether the same class of issue reappears. If malformed experiences cannot be reintroduced or classify no longer consumes raw exported experiences, this objection is false.

objections.claim: The proposal's "content is already captured" rationale is not machine-backed in the current substrate. The provenance graph only follows `source_experiences` links (`site/patron/src/components/MindTab.jsx:239-273`). In the current snapshot, `pattern:session:productive-holding` contains only `pattern` and `strength` (`/home/swami/swayambhu/dev-loop/overnight-2026-04-06-v2/runs/2026-04-07T00-52-26-491Z/context.json:316-318`), and `tactic:lengthen-interval-when-holding` contains only `source_principles` (`/home/swami/swayambhu/dev-loop/overnight-2026-04-06-v2/runs/2026-04-07T00-52-26-491Z/context.json:14119-14127`). The legacy record still holds the explicit waiting-state evidence (`/home/swami/swayambhu/dev-loop/overnight-2026-04-06-v2/runs/2026-04-07T00-52-26-491Z/context.json:14077-14085`). Deleting it now removes the only explicit machine-readable waiting-posture artifact instead of preserving that lineage in linked pattern/tactic state.
objections.falsifiable_test: Inspect the live KV for `pattern:session:productive-holding`, `tactic:lengthen-interval-when-holding`, and any other claimed replacement entity. If one of them already carries `source_experiences` linking back to `experience:session_4_waiting_posture` (or to another equivalent waiting-posture experience that preserves the same evidence), this objection is false.

objections.claim: The dev-loop deep-reflect comparison harness is another real downstream consumer the proposal ignores. `scripts/dev-loop/dr-compare.mjs:146-149` ingests every `experience:*` key through `compactExperience()`, and `scripts/dev-loop/dr-compare.mjs:270-290` increments raw `experienceCount` for each one and feeds that count into the `bootstrapLike` heuristic. That heuristic changes scoring behavior at `scripts/dev-loop/dr-compare.mjs:444-464` and `scripts/dev-loop/dr-compare.mjs:537-585`. Deleting `experience:session_4_waiting_posture` therefore changes comparison-harness inputs and potentially its scoring path, even though `memory.js:55-60` already filters non-canonical experiences out of core DR selection.
objections.falsifiable_test: Build a DR-compare snapshot with the key present and another with it removed, then compare `experienceCount`, `bootstrapLike`, and `scoreDrPayload()` output. If those values remain identical for the relevant snapshot, this objection is false.

overall_assessment: I found no runtime code path that references `experience:session_4_waiting_posture` by explicit key name, and I found no loader that requires this specific key to exist. The core readers enumerate by prefix and guard missing values (`reflect.js:403-408`, `dashboard-api/worker.js:202-206,289-297`), so I do not have a grounded null-reference objection. The schema mismatch is causing limited active harm: core DR silently skips the record (`memory.js:55-60`), `/mind` hides it unless it has canonical observation/narrative/action fields (`dashboard-api/worker.js:202-206`), but raw-count and analysis tooling still sees it. The grounded challenge to the proposal is therefore not "this will crash runtime"; it is that deletion is an incomplete fix that changes dashboard/dev-loop metrics, removes a live malformed-data signal, and drops the only explicit waiting-posture provenance without first migrating that provenance into linked canonical state.
